name: Ascent to the summit of Mondúver via the Coll de la Rafaela viewpoint — circular route around the Mondúver massif
name_original: Subida a la cima del Mondúver por el mirador del Coll de la Rafaela — circular por el macizo del Mondúver
region_intro: Hiking around Xeresa offers diverse terrain for outdoor enthusiasts, situated near the prominent Mondúver massif and within the scenic Safor region. The area is characterized by rugged landscapes, including notable cliffs like the Cingles de la Dona, and provides a range of trails. These features contribute to panoramic vistas and varied hiking experiences, from coastal views to mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Xeresa The most popular hiking route is La Font del Mondúver Trail (PR-CV 153), a 9.2 miles (14.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route features demanding climbs and panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to the summit of Mondúver via the Coll de la Rafaela viewpoint — circular route around the Mondúver massif, a difficult 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This circular route offers significant elevation gain and views from the Coll de la Rafaela viewpoint. Local hikers also love the Racó de la Ferradura de Xeresa — circular, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through an area of high scenic value, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Xeresa is defined by the prominent Mondúver massif, the spectacular Cingles de la Dona cliffs, and the rugged beauty of the Safor region. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Xeresa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Xeresa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the different difficulty levels for hiking trails around Xeresa?
A: Xeresa offers a range of trails primarily catering to moderate and difficult levels. There are 9 moderate routes and 3 difficult routes available, providing options for hikers with varying fitness levels. While there are no easy routes listed, many moderate paths can still be enjoyed by those looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes available in Xeresa?
A: Yes, Xeresa features several excellent circular hiking routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374325270/ascent-to-the-summit-of-monduver-via-the-coll-de-la-rafaela-viewpoint-circular-route-around-the-monduver-massif" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the summit of Mondúver via the Coll de la Rafaela viewpoint — circular route around the Mondúver massif&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356884452/raco-de-la-ferradura-de-xeresa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Racó de la Ferradura de Xeresa — circular&lt;/a&gt;, known for its high scenic value, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352603502/el-picaio-from-xeresa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Picaio from Xeresa — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a moderate challenge and good views.

Q: What kind of viewpoints or natural landmarks can I expect to see on Xeresa's trails?
A: The trails around Xeresa are rich in spectacular viewpoints and natural features. You can encounter the prominent Mondúver massif, offering challenging climbs and rewarding vistas. The Cingles de la Dona cliffs are renowned for their breathtaking panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3981973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Radella Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/635104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Visteta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; are also notable spots to take in the scenery.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Xeresa?
A: The hiking routes in Xeresa are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging ascents, and the rewarding panoramic views of the surrounding landscape, especially from areas like the Mondúver massif and Cingles de la Dona.

Q: Are there any historical sites or other attractions near the hiking routes in Xeresa?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, there are several historical sites and attractions nearby. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducal Palace of the Borgias (Palau Ducal dels Borja)&lt;/a&gt; in Gandia, or explore castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4027411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bairén Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/725112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Palma&lt;/a&gt;, which add a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Xeresa?
A: Given Xeresa's Mediterranean climate, the best times for hiking are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, allowing you to fully enjoy the rugged landscapes and panoramic views without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Xeresa?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Xeresa region, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage at trailheads and ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb wildlife or other hikers.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for families with children in Xeresa?
A: Most of the trails around Xeresa are rated as moderate to difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and rugged terrain. While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the guide, families with older, more experienced children who are accustomed to hiking might enjoy shorter moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View – Spring loop from Xeresa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views over a manageable distance.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails in Xeresa?
A: Parking for hiking trails in Xeresa is generally available near the starting points of the routes, often in or around the town itself. For specific trails, it's recommended to check the route details on komoot or local information boards, as designated parking areas can vary. Many routes, especially those starting directly from Xeresa, will have accessible parking options.

Q: What is the highest point I can reach on a hike in Xeresa?
A: The highest point you can reach on a hike in Xeresa is the summit of the Mondúver massif. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374325270/ascent-to-the-summit-of-monduver-via-the-coll-de-la-rafaela-viewpoint-circular-route-around-the-monduver-massif" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the summit of Mondúver via the Coll de la Rafaela viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; will take you to this prominent peak, offering incredible panoramic views of the surrounding Safor region and beyond.

Q: Are there any mountain passes to explore around Xeresa?
A: Yes, the region around Xeresa features several mountain passes that are popular for their scenic beauty and challenging terrain. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6773586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de la Drova&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3968468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Drova Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which offer rewarding climbs and descents, often with stunning views.
tips: There can be strong winds on the hill in winter. There is a shelter very close that is very pleasant on sunny days. The views are incredible on both sides of the hill.

A little detour to admire the 360 view and the rock with a hole. The trail is a little grown up, but feasible with a few scratches (May 2023)

The views from the Cingles de la Dona, located near Xeresa and Gandía in the province of Valencia, are a true treasure for hiking and nature lovers. Although there are no officially marked viewpoints, there are several spots along hiking trails from which you can enjoy spectacular panoramic views.
